syllabus.topic: Bayesian Decision Theory Foundations and MAP Classification
sub_points: Decision-theoretic formulation of classification: states/classes, actions/decisions, and loss (cost) functions
Bayes’ rule review and decomposition into prior, likelihood, evidence, and posterior; connection to MAP decision rule
Bayes optimal decision rule as minimizing conditional risk; relation between MAP and minimum expected loss
Special cases in classification: zero-one loss (MAP/maximum posterior), cost-sensitive decisions, and reject-option thresholds

syllabus.topic: Evaluation via Confusion Matrices and Threshold-Based Performance Curves
sub_points: Confusion matrix construction and derived metrics (e.g., accuracy, TPR/FPR, precision/recall, specificity)
ROC curve: generating ROC points by sweeping thresholds; interpreting AUC and operating points
Impact of class imbalance on ROC interpretation and why alternative metrics may be needed
Precision–Recall (PR) curve: when PR is preferable, relationship to object detection evaluation, and F-score summaries
Probabilistic prediction setup: predicting calibrated probabilities vs. hard labels and how decisions depend on thresholds/costs
KL divergence as a measure of discrepancy between distributions and its role in assessing probabilistic models
Entropy for (discrete) random variables as uncertainty; interpreting entropy in prediction and coding terms
Mutual information as dependence measure; interpretation and worked example; linkage between entropy and mutual information

syllabus.topic: Frequentist Decision Theory, Risk Minimization, and Model Selection
sub_points: Frequentist vs. Bayesian viewpoints; frequentist decision theory framing for learning from data
Risk concepts: Bayes risk, Bayes estimator, and worst-case (maximum) risk perspectives
Empirical Risk Minimization (ERM): objective, generalization gap, and performance behavior of ERM-fitted functions
Regularization and regularized risk; Occam’s razor as a principle motivating complexity control
Estimating population risk and selecting models with validation and cross-validation (including a worked example)
